name: meme-factory
description: Generate memes using the memegen.link API. Use when users request memes, want to add humor to content, or need visual aids for social media. Supports 100+ popular templates with custom text and styling.
capabilities: Generate memes using 100+ popular templates via memegen.link API
Format text with proper URL encoding for special characters
Create markdown-embedded images for documentation and chat
Suggest appropriate templates based on context (success, failure, comparison, etc.)
Support custom dimensions, layouts, fonts, and background images
Generate contextual memes for code reviews, deployments, and documentation
author: softaworks
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation with code examples using backticks for markdown formatting, public API URLs for the legitimate memegen.link service, and no actual shell commands or cryptographic operations. The Python helper script uses only standard library URL encoding for meme generation.
valueStatement: Users need engaging visual content for conversations, documentation, and social media. This skill generates contextual memes using the free memegen.link API with 100+ templates and custom text styling.
limitations: Depends on external memegen.link API availability
No built-in image editing or custom template creation
Meme text is limited to two lines (top and bottom)
Requires internet access for meme generation

useCases.title: Add humor to conversations
useCases.description: Generate relevant memes to lighten discussions, celebrate wins, or acknowledge challenges in a friendly way.

useCases.targetUser: Developers, technical writers, open source maintainers
useCases.title: Create engaging documentation
useCases.description: Generate contextual memes for README files, pull requests, and technical documentation to make content more engaging.

useCases.targetUser: Social media managers, community managers, content creators
useCases.title: Social media content
useCases.description: Create branded or themed memes for social media posts, team communications, and community engagement.

promptTemplates.title: Basic meme generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a meme using the {template} template with "{top_text}" on top and "{bottom_text}" at the bottom. Return the markdown image link.
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants a specific meme with custom text

promptTemplates.title: Context-based meme
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a meme for this situation: "{situation}". Suggest an appropriate template and generate the meme.
promptTemplates.scenario: When user describes a situation and needs a relevant meme

promptTemplates.title: Code review meme
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a meme for a {outcome} code review where {reason}. Use an appropriate template and include the markdown embed.
promptTemplates.scenario: Responding to code review outcomes with humor

promptTemplates.title: Deployment status meme
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a deployment {status} meme where the deployment {outcome}. Use the success template for successful deploys or the fine template for issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Sharing deployment status updates with the team

bestPractices: Keep meme text concise (2-6 words per line for readability)
Match the template to the message context (success, failure, comparison, etc.)
Use proper URL encoding for special characters (underscores for spaces, ~q for ?)
antiPatterns: Using spaces without encoding (URL will break)
Using the wrong template for the context (message loses impact)

faq.question: What templates are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports 100+ templates including buzz, drake, success, fine, fry, changemind, distracted, yodawg, interesting, mordor, and many more.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: No, memegen.link is a free, open API that does not require authentication or API keys.

faq.question: Can I use custom images?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use any image as a custom background by passing a style URL parameter with the custom image URL.

faq.question: What image formats are supported?
faq.answer: PNG (default, best quality), JPG (smaller file size), WebP (modern compression), and GIF (animated templates).

faq.question: How are special characters handled?
faq.answer: Spaces become underscores, question marks become ~q, slashes become ~s, and other special characters have specific encodings.

faq.question: Can I customize dimensions for different platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, use width and height parameters. Common sizes: 1200x630 for social media, 800x600 for Slack/Discord.
